sourceLabels: Project Planning And Design
folders.instructions: The Project Planning and Design folder is organized by tranche. Use the highest tranche number as the most recent unless the user explicitly asks for an earlier tranche. Prefer Concept Note Guidelines for concept note questions and Project Document Guidelines for project document questions. Do not use blank templates unless the user specifically asks for templates. Use Excel annexes for annex-table questions together with Project Document Guidelines. Use review checklists only for QA/QC, internal review, or pre-submission review questions.

sourceLabels: Evaluations
folders.instructions: Use the Evaluation knowledge folder. When a document is tranche-labeled, use the highest tranche number unless the user explicitly asks about an earlier tranche. Use non-tranche general evaluation documents where they apply across tranches. Use guidance notes and annotated templates as primary sources for substantive answers. Do not use unannotated templates unless the user specifically asks for a blank template or template structure. The DA guidance note on terminal evaluation of joint projects v1 is relevant from T12 onward. The DA project evaluation report template 02-2026 and DA suggested management response template 02-2026 are relevant from T15 onward.

sourceLabels: Project Planning And Design
folders.instructions: The general folder is organized by tranche. Use the highest tranche number as the most recent unless the user explicitly asks for an earlier tranche. Prefer Concept Note Guidelines for concept note questions and Project Document Guidelines for project document questions. Do not use blank templates unless the user specifically asks for templates. Use Excel annexes for annex-table questions together with Project Document Guidelines. Use review checklists only for QA/QC, internal review, or pre-submission review questions.

sourceLabels: Project Planning And Design
folders.instructions: This folder contains all historical meetings of the Capacity Development Steering Committee. Items in this folder could answer questions like What was decided in the steering committee meeting in a certain year or draw conclusions from multiple meetings.

sourceLabels: TAG Meetings
folders.instructions: This folder contains all historical meetings of the Capacity Development Tag Team. Items in this folder could answer questions like What was decided in the TAG meeting in a certain year or draw conclusions from multiple meetings.

sourceLabels: Project Evaluations 4 Cj 3 N 0 N Ll 1 O 8 Se
folders.instructions: This folder contains an excel file with all project evaluation files. The excel schema contains the following information about the evaluations: Title Division(s) EvaluationYear Countries RecommendationsList LessonsLearnedList FileName ShortPath FullPath. Use this folder for questions about specific evaluations or evaluation reports, including finding, listing, counting, filtering, or retrieving evaluations by topic, title, country, countries, region, division, year, recommendation, lesson learned, project attribute, FileName, ShortPath, or FullPath. When the user asks how many reports or evaluations match criteria, count matching records from the evaluation file if possible and cite the file. When the user asks to find evaluations about a subject or country, return the most relevant matching evaluation titles with the fields that support the match.

sourceLabels: Knowledge WY Tp Gld A Wsk 7 O 4 C 6 Vn
folders.instructions: These uploaded documents provide templates and guidance for UN Development Account-style project design, monitoring, reporting, budgeting, and closure. Use this knowledge for questions about preparing 2025 annual progress reports, including reporting deadlines, project metadata, implementation rates, revisions, indicators of achievement, output delivery, supplementary funding, staff resources, Resident Coordinator Office involvement, contractor engagement, and country-specific action plans; final report templates covering project features, results narratives, performance indicators, implemented activities, deliverables, challenges, good practices, sustainability, multiplier effects, supplementary funding, and financial information; older generic progress report templates focused on achievements, performance indicators, activity status, supplementary funding, challenges, revisions, and expenditure/implementation-rate tracking; and project-document preparation guidance for designing a development project, including background, SDG alignment, problem analysis, stakeholder/capacity assessment, gender and disability analysis, logical framework, theory of change, risks, governance, sustainability, communications, workplan, and budget justification.

sourceLabels: Knowledge WY Tp Gld A Wsk 7 O 4 C 6 Vn
folders.instructions: This folder provides DESA's 25 September 2018 interim guidance and working templates for using grants and implementing partner agreements. Use this knowledge for questions about when to use a grant, implementing partner modality, or procurement; definitions of implementing partners, grants, and vendors; procedures for identifying, screening, selecting, approving, amending, monitoring, closing, and keeping records for grantees and implementing partners; and the roles of DESA divisions, CDO, OCSS/Procurement, IPGC, OLA, and the Controller's Office in due diligence and agreement approval. The annexes file supplies operational templates needed to apply those guidelines, including a routing slip to CDO, a project cooperation agreement template, required agreement clauses on funds, reporting, audits, anti-fraud/anti-corruption, termination, intellectual property, equipment, and privileges/immunities, plus a due-diligence questionnaire, IPGC decision template, partner evaluation template, and a reference table distinguishing activities that normally fit implementing-partner arrangements versus procurement.
